document.title: Software Development Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software development plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software, including classification, development process, requirements, risk management, verification and validation, release, configuration management, and problem handling.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as technical documentation supporting software development controls and verification for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
keyPoints: The plan states that software is developed to manipulate and utilize the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector product functions.
Software classification is addressed under FDA level of concern and EN 62304 software safety class concepts.
The development process follows a classic waterfall methodology with analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, maintenance, software release, and development plan update stages.
Software verification and validation are confirmed through software tests including unit test, integration, and system test.
Software release requires confirmation that verification is completed, residual abnormalities are documented and assessed, and release version and distribution procedures are documented.

concepts.name: Software Development Plan
concepts.evidence: Devise a plan for the software development and put it in writing

concepts.name: FDA Level of Concern
concepts.pageTarget: Software Classification - FDA Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

concepts.name: EN 62304 Software Safety Class
concepts.evidence: The software safety classes shall initially be assigned based on concern as follows: -. Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

concepts.name: Classic waterfall methodology
concepts.evidence: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

concepts.name: Software Risk Management
concepts.evidence: As for the software risk management, personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

concepts.name: Software Verification and Validation
concepts.evidence: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

concepts.name: Software Configuration Management
concepts.evidence: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

concepts.name: Cyber Security
concepts.evidence: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

evidence.claim: The document is a software development plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-10-143 with Rev.0 stated in the document body.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The revision history records Rev. 0 dated 2024.07.01 as the initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. 0 Revision date 2024.07.01 The reason & content for establishment and amendment Initial Release

evidence.claim: The purpose is to develop software that manipulates and utilizes the product function of the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: To use the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector developed, produced and sold by this company according to intention, it aims at developing software that can manipulate and utilize the product function.

evidence.claim: Software classification is addressed according to FDA guideline levels of concern: Major, Moderate, and Minor.
evidence.evidenceText: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern
evidence.pageTarget: Software Classification - FDA Level of Concern

evidence.claim: EN 62304 software safety classes are Class A, Class B, and Class C based on possible harm.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

evidence.claim: If a software system has not yet been assigned a software safety class, Class C requirements apply.
evidence.evidenceText: For each software system, until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements applied.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

evidence.claim: The development schedule includes analysis from 2024.07.01 to 2024.07.15 and software release from 2024.11.15 to 2024.11.22.
The software development environment identifies Windows 11 64bit as the operating system.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation system Windows 11 64bit

evidence.claim: The development compiler resources include Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional, aarch64-poky-linux-gcc, and C#.
evidence.evidenceText: Compiler Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional / aarch64-poky-linux-gcc / C#

evidence.claim: The MCU model identified in the hardware resources is Intel Cyclone V.
evidence.evidenceText: MCU Model Intel Cyclone V

evidence.claim: Functional software requirements include common functions, image acquisition, calibration, pre-processing, and firmware update modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions Module ... Image acquisition Module ... Calibration Module ... Pre-Processing Module ... Firmware Update Module

evidence.claim: Firmware functions include xSystem, FPGA App, System task, and Interface modules.
evidence.evidenceText: xSystem Module ... FPGA App Module ... System task Module ... Interface Module

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include limiting wireless network access by setting ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include protecting transmission data with encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, and WPAPSK+TKIP.
evidence.evidenceText: Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Software risk analysis is linked to EN ISO14971:2019 in the verification activities table.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk analysis Identification of the characteristics and hazards associated with the intended use and the safety of the device, risk assessment EN ISO14971:2019 Software Validation Report Quality manager

evidence.claim: Software development planning is linked to EN ISO13485:2016 in the verification activities table.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development Devise a plan for the software development and put it in writing EN ISO13485:2016 Software development plan R&D manager

evidence.claim: Software verification and validation are confirmed through software testing including unit, integration, and system tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

evidence.claim: Software release requires confirmation that verification is completed and results are evaluated before release.
evidence.evidenceText: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

evidence.claim: Identified residual abnormalities must be documented and evaluated to ensure they do not cause unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented. In order to ensure that all identified residual abnormalities are not causing unacceptable risks, It should be ensured that such abnormal conditions have been evaluated.

evidence.claim: Software configuration changes must be recorded in the Software Validation Report and validated before application.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be recorded, managed on 12. Software Configuration Management of the Software Validation Report. In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

evidence.claim: Software issues must be recorded and applicable corrective measures conducted.
evidence.evidenceText: Any issues identified with the software shall be recorded and applicable corrective measures shall be conducted.

reviewFlags.reason: Revision metadata is conflicting: sourcePath filename states Software Development Plan_Rev.1, while document text states Report No.: A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.0) and revision history Rev. 0.
Authority or jurisdiction is ambiguous in the document body: source metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, while the text references FDA Guide Line and EN 62304 without explicitly naming MFDS.
No explicit product model numbers are stated in the document body beyond G Series and Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
The source text appears to be extracted from a formatted document; some tables are flattened and checkbox answers are not filled.
